meta.title: mantabridge Guide: Manta Pacific Bridge
meta.description: Independent mantabridge guide covering Manta Pacific deposits, Atlantic routes, fees, third-party options, safety checks, and official links.
meta.og_description: Bridge assets to Manta Pacific with route, fee, wallet, and risk checks in plain English.

hero.lead: Bridge supported assets into the Manta ecosystem while checking the route, wallet, gas, and trust model before signing.

widget.note: Illustrative bridge preview only; no wallet connection here.

what_is.html: Mantabridge usually refers to bridge flows for Manta Network, especially the official Manta Pacific bridge from Ethereum to Manta Pacific. Manta describes itself as a modular ecosystem for high-performance decentralized applications in its official docs. Use this as an independent guide, not an official app, and verify the domain before connecting a wallet.

sections.intro: The main Manta Pacific bridge flow starts with Ethereum Mainnet as the source and Manta Pacific Mainnet as the destination. Exact token support, timing, and cost components can change in the live interface.

steps.d: Use Manta Network, the official Manta docs, or the Manta Pacific bridge URL, then confirm the domain before connecting a wallet.
Select deposit or withdraw, source network, destination network, token, and amount. Keep the native gas token available on the chain that signs the transaction.
Check token approval, source-chain gas, destination execution or claim gas, and any route-specific bridge or third-party fee shown by the interface.
After signing, follow the bridge history or block explorer status and do not repeat transactions just because a wallet balance updates slowly.

sections.intro: There is no universal mantabridge fee or arrival time. Costs and risk depend on gas markets, the selected path, token approvals, bridge contracts, and any third-party provider involved.

cards.p_html: Expect source-chain gas, token approval gas when required, destination gas or claim cost when required, and any route fee displayed before signing. The official Manta Pacific Bridge is the place to review the current quote.

Bridge designs vary: some paths rely on native rollup contracts, while third-party routes may use liquidity networks, relayers, or other verification models. Ethereum.org's bridge overview explains why bridge trust assumptions matter.
Manta Pacific is an Ethereum L2 network with its own chain settings and explorer. The official network information lists the chain ID, RPC endpoints, explorer links, and ETH gas symbol.

sections.intro: Manta has more than one bridge-related surface, so match the guide you are reading to the network you actually intend to use.

cards.p_html: The public Pacific bridge interface shows Ethereum Mainnet to Manta Pacific Mainnet for deposits and includes a withdraw tab. Start from pacific-bridge.manta.network or Manta's main site rather than search ads.
Manta Pacific is the EVM L2, while Manta Atlantic is the Polkadot-based network used for identity and credential use cases. Manta's introduction docs explain both networks.

The Manta Pacific bridge page also lists independent third-party providers. Manta says those services are linked for convenience and are not operated by Manta, so read the provider's own docs and use the official page to verify context.

bands.title: Manta Bridge Routes to Check
bands.intro: Use these route labels to verify that the source chain, destination chain, wallet type, and token form match your goal.

cards.name: Manta Pacific Bridge
cards.intent: Use this for the official Manta Pacific deposit and withdraw interface. Confirm the URL, asset, amount, gas, and destination before signing.

cards.name: Manta Atlantic Bridge
cards.intent: Use this only when you need the Atlantic-side app and a compatible Polkadot-style wallet. Check current Manta migration notices before relying on Atlantic routes.

cards.name: MANTA Pacific to Atlantic
cards.intent: Use Manta's docs when moving MANTA between Pacific and Atlantic. The flow can involve both an EVM wallet and an Atlantic wallet.

cards.name: Third-Party Bridge List
cards.intent: Use only after reading the provider's own route, fee, and security details. A listing on the bridge page does not make the provider part of Manta.

bands.title: Assets and Networks Users Check
bands.intro: Availability can change, so treat these as items to verify in the live bridge screen before moving funds.

cards.name: Ethereum Mainnet
cards.intent: Use Ethereum Mainnet routes when funds start on L1. Keep enough ETH for approvals and the source transaction.

cards.name: Manta Pacific
cards.intent: Use Manta Pacific when the destination app expects assets on the Manta L2. Confirm chain ID and explorer details from official docs.

cards.name: Ether
cards.intent: ETH is shown in the Manta Pacific bridge interface and is also the gas symbol on Manta Pacific. Verify the exact route output before sending.

cards.name: MANTA Token
cards.intent: Use MANTA bridge docs when moving the token across Manta networks. Do not assume exchange deposits support every network version.

cards.name: Manta Explorer
cards.intent: Use the explorer to inspect Manta Pacific transactions after the source transaction is submitted. Keep the transaction hash and wallet address handy.

compare.title: Official bridge vs third-party route
compare.intro_html: Manta's Pacific bridge page links both the official bridge surface and independent providers. Ethereum.org's bridge guide is a useful neutral primer on why those models can differ.
rows: Supported tokens, gas, withdrawal path, bridge status
Users moving between Ethereum and Manta Pacific
Wallet type, migration notices, token direction, network status
Provider docs, liquidity, fees, contract risk, support channels
Users comparing alternate routes into Manta

faq.q: Is mantabridge the official Manta Bridge?
faq.a_html: Mantabridge is a search term, not a guarantee that a page is official. The official Manta site links to the Pacific bridge at pacific-bridge.manta.network and Manta's broader app at app.manta.network. Verify the domain before connecting a wallet.

Manta Pacific is Manta Network's EVM-compatible Ethereum L2 for scalable decentralized applications. Manta's official introduction describes Pacific as the L2 side of the Manta ecosystem, separate from Manta Atlantic. That distinction matters when choosing a bridge route and wallet.

faq.q: What fees apply when using mantabridge?
faq.a_html: Fees are components, not one fixed number: source-chain gas, token approval gas when needed, destination gas or claim cost when required, and any bridge or third-party provider fee shown before signing. Review the current quote in the official Manta Pacific bridge interface.

Every bridge has risk. Users should account for smart-contract risk, wrong-network transfers, wallet approvals, phishing, route downtime, and third-party provider assumptions. Ethereum.org's bridge overview explains common bridge trust models and risks in neutral terms.

faq.q: Can I bridge MANTA between Manta Pacific and Manta Atlantic?
faq.a_html: Manta docs include instructions for bridging MANTA between Manta Pacific and Manta Atlantic, including using both an Atlantic wallet and a Pacific wallet in the flow. Read the current native bridge guide before starting.

faq.q: How do I verify a Manta Pacific transaction?
faq.a_html: Use the official Manta Pacific explorer link from Manta's network information page, then search by transaction hash or address. The network information docs list explorer URLs, RPC endpoints, chain ID, and other details needed to confirm you are on the right network.

footer_note: Independent educational guide, not affiliated with Manta Network; verify official links and current bridge details before moving funds.
